lesson: Initial duplicate training passed by exact file copy and PDF visual parity. This does not prove from-scratch creation or semantic Gantt update ability; future runs must test structured edits.
Controlled update training shows bars can be moved by shifting cell fills/blank-space values and preserving borders/alignment. The workbook uses cell-based Gantt visuals, not embedded chart objects.
Semantic update v2 failed because old bars were cleared before style was copied, leaving the moved bars with no fill. Always capture source style before clearing a row.

Semantic update v3 fixed existing moved rows but the new row style source referenced a cell that had already been cleared in the same pass. Capture a known-good style sample before any update and reuse that for new rows.
Semantic update v4 passed: timeline headers were mapped to columns, date text drove bar placement, existing bars and a new row were styled correctly, and Excel PDF visual comparison stayed localised.
From-blank rebuild v1 failed because internal openpyxl _style ids were copied across workbooks. Copy style objects instead of style ids.
From-blank rebuild v2 fixed theme colours by copying the source workbook theme palette. Without the theme, bars rendered in the wrong colours even when fill objects were copied.

From-blank rebuild v3/v4 exposed xlsx package pitfalls: append-writing replacement XML creates duplicate zip members, and hidden legacyDrawing references can conflict with inserted drawings. Rewrite a clean package and remove stale drawing refs.
From-blank rebuild v5 passed with zero rendered-pixel difference after preserving theme, header/footer OFFICIAL marks, the red today-line drawing, VML, relationships, and clean xlsx packaging.
